mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with statistical significance (p < 0.05) over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, it signals a breaking labor market and imminent recessionary pressures, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high consumer price inflation and declining retail sales indicate demand destruction and stagflationary pressures, forcing equity valuations lower as corporate margins compress over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint high-CPI and low-RSAFS periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, a sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening economic activity that compels central banks to pause rate hikes, generating positive short-term equity rebounds.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 70% of instances where INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or regime-conditional IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor demand and sustained consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during payroll expansions with p < 0.05 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.5 million units, it signals a severe housing market downturn that drags on construction activity and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where HOUST drops below 1500, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in federal funds rates and the unemployment rate confirms a policy-induced economic slowdown, triggering recession fears that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint rising FEDFUNDS and UNRATE periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, quarterly GDP growth below 1.0 percent signals demand exhaustion that forces corporate earnings revisions downward, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0 percent during high-inflation periods, or regime-conditional IC < 0.01.
